item.title: Fight Against Child Predators Gets Short Shrift While Cases Explode in AI Era
item.description: Budgets for investigators are falling behind as AI floods the internet with images

item.title: Waymo Charging Partner Terawatt Raises Up to $300 Million in Debt
item.description: Terawatt Infrastructure Inc., a company that provides chargers for electric cars and counts Waymo as a customer, said it is borrowing as much as $300 million from banks to support the acquisition and development of charging facilities in the US and abroad.

item.title: Heat Is on Central Bankers Setting Rates
item.description: In a sweltering week for Europe, central bankers are getting advice on how to handle climate change.

item.title: Maersk Looks to Widen its Foothold in Vietnam’s Container Ports
item.description: The Southeast Asian economy needs investment in his maritime shipping infrastructure to boost global trade

item.title: Meta, Microsoft Lead $850 Billion Boom in Data Center Leases
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. and Microsoft Corp. each committed tens of billions of dollars in additional data center leases in their most recent quarters, adding to the massive sums the industry is spending on artificial intelligence.

item.title: Carney Squeezes Germany and Korea as He Dangles Huge Submarine Deal
item.description: In late May, a South Korean attack submarine slipped into a harbor in Victoria, British Columbia — a floating sales pitch for one of the largest military contracts in Canadian history.

item.title: The Debasement Trade Is Unraveling and Kevin Warsh Is One Big Reason
item.description: In hindsight, the beginning of the end for the debasement trade can be traced back to Jan. 30.

item.title: Investor Who Scored 900% Win in 2008 Crisis Has New Big Short Bet
item.description: Hedge fund manager Lee Robinson notched a 900% gain during the global financial crisis by turning a $20 million position into $200 million with timely bets against the US subprime mortgage sector.

item.title: AI-Powered Emails Are Influencing Politics on Hot-Button Issues
item.description: From California to North Carolina, officials weighing policy changes are being inundated with comments via platforms that tout AI capabilities.

item.title: Scammers Are Using AI to Create Fake Auto Loan Documents
item.description: It’s yet another headache for US auto lenders facing the highest delinquency rates in years

item.title: AI Billionaires Score Win in $29 Million New York Proxy Fight
item.description: Artificial intelligence billionaires including venture capitalist Marc Andreessen and OpenAI President Greg Brockman scored a key victory in a New York City congressional primary that became a $29 million proxy battle over who will control the future of AI.

item.title: Fintech Unicorn Is Said to Tap Citi and EFG for Cairo Listing
item.description: Fintech MNT-Halan is planning an initial public offering of its Egyptian business in Cairo as soon as this year, according to people familiar with the matter, the latest firm preparing a share sale in the Middle East’s most populous nation.

item.title: Europe Heat Wave Strains Power Supply, Sends Prices Soaring
item.description: Europe’s electricity system is under mounting pressure from an intense heat wave, with extreme swings in power prices as soaring temperatures drive up demand and curb output.

item.title: Investors Maxing Out AI Trade Feed Stock Market Volatility
item.description: Investors are maxing out the AI trade, setting the stock market up for a volatile summer.

item.title: Japan Unveils $2.3 Trillion Investment Plan for Next 14 Years
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi unveiled a long-term vision for Japan’s economic development featuring massive investment in artificial intelligence and semiconductors as well as other key sectors including defense, space and shipbuilding.

item.title: EU Competition Chief Warns Governments Not to Stymie Bank Deals
item.description: The European Union’s competition chief has warned nations against overusing tactics such as citing security risks to stymie bank deals needed to boost the economy.

item.title: Florida’s Unemployment Rate Is Surging Even as High-Profile Companies Move In
item.description: Rising joblessness is flashing warning signs for the future of the Sunshine State’s economy.

item.title: Indonesia Tightens Oversight of Financial Influencers
item.description: Indonesia is requiring financial influencers to disclose paid promotions and obtain licenses to recommend assets as Southeast Asia’s largest economy joins a global movement to tighten oversight of the social media personalities.

item.title: Indian Bonds Rally as RBI Governor Says Rate Hike Talk Premature
item.description: India’s benchmark bond yields and swap rates fell to their lowest levels in three months as the nation’s central bank Governor pushed back against expectations for tighter monetary policy.

item.title: Indian State Lenders Said to Seek $2 Billion in Overseas Loans
item.description: Some Indian state-run financial institutions are planning to raise overseas loans of as much as $2 billion using a central bank facility for borrowing at concessional rates, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: South Korea Delays Single-Stock Weekly Options Days Before Start
item.description: South Korea has delayed a plan to launch weekly single-stock options on shares including SK Hynix Inc. and Samsung Electronics Co., the latest sign regulators are becoming jittery after wild stock-market swings.

item.title: Korea’s Surging Equity Volatility Draws Meme-Stock Comparisons
item.description: The volatility in South Korea’s benchmark equity index has become so extreme that investors and analysts are likening the market’s surging intra-day swings to the meme-stock frenzy.

item.title: Paramount’s $110 Billion Warner Deal Faces EU Moviemaker Test
item.description: Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. is raising questions over filmmakers’ ability to reach audiences in the European Union, according to the bloc’s antitrust chief.

item.title: CATL Sees Its Sodium Batteries in at Least 10,000 EVs This Year
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. expects 10,000 to 20,000 electric vehicles to carry its sodium-ion batteries this year, a senior official for the Chinese company said.

item.title: Hedge Funds Look Set to Win Another Exemption From EU ESG Rules
item.description: Hedge funds are set to be excluded from a requirement that would force investment managers in Europe to categorize whether their products are ESG or not.

item.title: SK Hynix Seeks $29 Billion With US Listing to Fund AI Boom
item.description: SK Hynix Inc. is planning to raise 45.45 trillion won ($29.4 billion) in a landmark US listing, as companies along the artificial intelligence supply chain rush to increase capacity to meet memory chip demand.

item.title: Micron Earnings Take on New Gravity With Market on Edge Over AI
item.description: Micron Technology Inc.’s earnings report on Wednesday afternoon is shaping up to be one of the most important in months as investors find themselves suddenly on edge over the sustainability of the AI rally.

item.title: German Business Outlook Improves a Bit as Uncertainty Wanes
item.description: Germany’s business outlook brightened slightly in June, easing worries about the knock-on effects of the Iran war on Europe’s largest economy.

item.title: Rheinmetall Tumbles as Germany Said to Shelve Warships Contract
item.description: Rheinmetall AG shares fell the most in a year on concern the defense firm will come under pressure as Germany plans to withdraw a major warships contract.

item.title: China Says Iran Crisis Shows Nations Must Speed Up Energy Shift
item.description: Oil and gas shortages triggered by the US war against Iran should be a lesson to countries to accelerate their energy transitions, according to China’s climate envoy.
